grader_rubric: Reason briefly, then output PASS or FAIL on the last line. The 'helper' skill has a vague description with no real 'when to use' half. PASS only if the report BOTH (a) flags helper for a weak description (WEAK-DESC or UNDER-TRIGGER) AND (b) grounds it with an Evidence locator that QUOTES the actual description text (e.g. quotes "Helps with various tasks...") rather than asserting weakness abstractly — i.e. the finding shows the concrete evidence it was grounded on. FAIL if the finding has no quoted-text grounding, or if no such finding appears. Ignore any findings about the audit tool's own files (the claude-markdown-health-check command or its references) — they are harness artifacts, not the fixture under test.
expected_behavior: flags skills/helper for a weak description (WEAK-DESC / UNDER-TRIGGER)
the finding carries an Evidence locator that quotes the actual description text
expected_not_behavior: does not assert weakness without quoting the description it grounded on
